metadata.title: Jairo González — Frontend Developer
metadata.description: Frontend Developer specialized in React, Next.js, and TypeScript. I build modern, accessible, high-performance interfaces.

hero.name: Jairo González
hero.taglineStart: I build accessible, fast interfaces with animations that tell a story. Specialized in

projectsSection.title: Things I've built
projectsSection.description: A selection of projects I've worked on over the last few years, from multi-tenant SaaS platforms to richly animated websites. Each one shows a different kind of work.

about.title: How I got here
paragraphs: I started by studying Pharmacy at the University of Havana, where I earned my degree. During those years I also completed two years of Computer Science, which I left for personal reasons — but my curiosity for programming stayed with me.

I picked it back up through self-directed learning and, over the last 2 years, I've built full web applications as a freelancer: multi-tenant SaaS platforms, tools with AI integrations, crypto dashboards, and websites with advanced animations.
Today I work from Madrid, specializing in frontend with React and Next.js, and I am available for remote or on-site projects. I care about interfaces that feel fast, accessible, and well designed.
about.stackLabel: Stack I use day to day

contact.description: Have a project in mind, an opening, or just want to say hello? Write to me and I'll reply in under 24 hours.

items.title: VOO Platform
items.subtitle: Multi-tenant SaaS frontend for e-commerce, bookings, and events
items.description: A multi-tenant SaaS platform that lets event, hospitality, and service businesses launch their own site in minutes. I built the full frontend: customer dashboard, global admin panel, public landing page, and a customizable template system serving multiple tenants from a single codebase.
highlights: Monorepo with 3 frontend apps and 5 shared packages (UI, auth, queries, emails)
Custom domain support with dynamic tenant routing
Multi-language support with next-intl and dynamic theming configurable per business
Deployment to Cloudflare Workers via OpenNext for low global latency

caseStudy.challenge: Build the frontend of a multi-tenant SaaS from scratch, allowing three very different verticals (events, hospitality, and services) to offer a personalized end-user experience without duplicating code, while keeping accessibility and performance at an enterprise-product level.
caseStudy.solution: I designed a Bun and Turborepo monorepo architecture with three independent applications (customer dashboard, global admin, and landing page) sharing UI, authentication, query, and email-template packages. The template system lets each tenant customize colors, typography, language, and content blocks without touching code.

decisions.title: UI composition with Radix + shadcn/ui
decisions.body: I built an accessible component system (WAI-ARIA) reused across the 3 apps, with semantic CSS-variable tokens to support tenant theming at runtime without recompilation.

decisions.title: Internationalization with next-intl
decisions.body: I integrated next-intl with subdomain segmentation to serve different languages depending on each tenant's region, with automatic fallback and pluralization support.

decisions.title: Edge deployment with Cloudflare Workers
decisions.body: I migrated deployment to Cloudflare Workers using @opennextjs/cloudflare to achieve sub-50ms global latency, eliminate cold starts, and reduce infrastructure cost.

decisions.title: End-to-end typing
decisions.body: I shared API-generated types (TypeBox/Prisma) across the frontend apps, eliminating contract errors and giving full autocomplete for every call.

caseStudy.outcome: The product now supports multiple tenants in production with load times under 1s and Lighthouse 90+ on mobile. The modular architecture makes it possible to add new verticals in days, not weeks.

items.title: AI Analyzer
items.subtitle: AI-generated content detection and humanization with a rich editor
items.description: A SaaS platform that analyzes text to detect AI-generated content and offers automatic humanization. It integrates 3 different models (Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, Winston AI) in a unified interface with a rich editor, credit system, and paid subscriptions.
highlights: Rich text editor (Novel/TipTap) with code highlighting and before/after comparison
Integration of 3 AI providers with a unified UI and runtime model selector
Subscription system with Stripe (checkout, webhook, customer portal)
Results visualization with interactive charts and PDF export

caseStudy.challenge: Design a user experience that made the output of three different AI models understandable and actionable for non-technical users, while keeping response times feeling fast despite the inherent latency of LLM calls.
caseStudy.solution: I built an interface centered on a rich editor as the main surface, presenting analysis results as interactive visualizations instead of raw output. The integration with Vercel AI SDK enables response streaming, giving users immediate feedback.

decisions.title: Streaming with Vercel AI SDK
decisions.body: I used the AI SDK to stream Claude/Gemini responses directly into the editor, removing the feeling of waiting and showing humanized content as it is generated.

decisions.title: Editor with Novel + TipTap extensions
decisions.body: I customized Novel with code highlighting extensions, paragraph-level AI-probability markers, and visual before/after comparison while preserving a familiar editor flow.

decisions.title: Stripe + credit system
decisions.body: I implemented checkout, webhooks, and the customer portal with Stripe, plus a credit system that deducts tokens based on model and text length, with clear visual limits in the UI.

decisions.title: Validation with Zod + React Hook Form
decisions.body: All user input (prompts, configuration, payment data) is validated on the client with Zod and React Hook Form, returning accessible inline errors before credits are spent.

caseStudy.outcome: The interface turned a technical product into something usable for content creators without an AI background. The architecture makes adding new AI providers possible with minimal frontend changes.

items.title: CubanParty
items.subtitle: Events platform with interactive animations
items.description: An event promotion and management platform with a strong visual layer: interactive particle animations, smooth page transitions, and a content-management system backed by Appwrite. All media is served from AWS S3 with intelligent preloading.
highlights: Particle animations with tsparticles synchronized to scroll
Smooth page transitions with Framer Motion (layout animations + AnimatePresence)
Serverless backend with Appwrite (auth, database, storage)
Media optimization with AWS S3 + viewport-based preloading

caseStudy.challenge: Create a memorable visual experience for an events platform without sacrificing performance on mobile devices, where most of the audience visits. The challenge was integrating complex particle animations with real-time data without frame drops.
caseStudy.solution: I combined tsparticles for background effects (with lightweight presets on mobile) and Framer Motion for UI transitions, clearly separating decorative animation from interactive motion. Data management with Appwrite + SWR keeps the app reactive without a custom backend.

decisions.title: Adaptive particle system
decisions.body: I implemented device capability detection to reduce particles and disable interactivity on mobile, keeping a consistent 60fps even on mid-range devices.

decisions.title: Appwrite as BaaS
decisions.body: I chose Appwrite over Firebase for its relational data model, cacheable REST API, and SDK that integrates cleanly with SWR for real-time data.

decisions.title: Layout animations with Framer Motion
decisions.body: I used layoutId for shared transitions between event lists and detail views, creating a cinematic feel without a performance penalty.

decisions.title: AWS S3 + on-the-fly transformations
decisions.body: I combined S3 with image transformations at the edge to serve modern formats (AVIF/WebP) based on the user's browser, reducing average image weight by 70%.

caseStudy.outcome: The platform achieved Lighthouse Performance 90+ on mobile despite the animations, and became a portfolio piece showing that performance and visual design can work together.

items.title: Memecoin Dashboard
items.subtitle: Token trading interface with real-time visualization
items.description: Dashboard for managing and visualizing crypto token transactions. Complex forms with real-time validation, price evolution charts, and responsive design optimized for long sessions on large screens.
highlights: Multi-step forms with React Hook Form and instant validation
Real-time data visualization with optimistic updates
Responsive design optimized for long desktop sessions
Contextual notification system with Radix Toast

items.title: Mojitos Bar
items.subtitle: Cocktail website with animated GSAP storytelling
items.description: A one-page cocktail bar website built as a showcase for advanced GSAP animations. Every section is choreographed with timelines and ScrollTriggers, demonstrating control over micro-interactions and visual storytelling.
highlights: GSAP timelines with ScrollTrigger in every section
Character-by-character text reveals synchronized with scroll
Multi-layer parallax and complex animation scrubbing
Responsive design that keeps its fluidity on mobile
